mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates eliminate discount rate headwinds for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns as capital flows into risk assets.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates crossing above 5.0% signal deteriorating labor market conditions that suppress corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for UNRATE crosses above 0.05 in 60-day forward returns when UNRATE > 5.0%, or positive returns occur in >60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital demand expansion, producing positive equity returns as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint positive correlation between RSAFS and HOUST growth rates fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >50% of overlapping monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index readings above 105 reflect robust manufacturing capacity utilization that supports short-term earnings momentum, yielding positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months where INDPRO > 105, or the signal loses predictive power during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels crossing above 230.0 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in >55% of months when CPIAUCSL > 230.0, or the inflation threshold shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate consumer spending capacity, creating a persistent positive drift in equity markets as corporate revenue growth accelerates.
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS growth shows a negative or null correlation with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP contractions trigger immediate risk-off positioning as investors price in recessionary earnings downgrades, producing negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following negative GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth are positive in >60% of cases, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero policy rates and elevated unemployment creates a dovish policy floor that sustains liquidity injections, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of low rates and high unemployment coincides with negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction term loses predictive significance.
